rationales.hedonism: He lingers over a pleasant family moment, 'sat on the balcony eating' tamales and noticing the jalapeno shoots, showing active enjoyment of comfort in the present.
rationales.achievement: He emphasizes delivering 'the payment gateway migration three days early' and compares his substantial work to a colleague's praised prototype, showing strong concern with competent performance and recognition.

rationales.self_direction: He suppresses his own judgment even after 'I ran the numbers over the weekend' because 'Diego is the architect,' yielding his independent view to hierarchy.
rationales.security: He chooses to 'document my concerns in the ticket comments so there's a paper trail' and says 'That way I'm covered,' prioritizing protection over confrontation.
rationales.conformity: He stays quiet because speaking up would 'make things awkward' and decides not to 'make it a thing,' showing restraint to preserve workplace smoothness.

rationales.achievement: He inventories concrete accomplishments like 'Seventeen production releases,' mentoring promotions, and cutting CI time, then plans to 'quantify everything' in his review.
rationales.conformity: Even though the self-assessment feels like 'wearing a costume that doesn't fit,' he decides to use 'the strong verbs' the system expects rather than resist the process.

rationales.achievement: He reframes his work in concrete, high-responsibility terms, from reverse-engineering legacy code to mentoring engineers, and completes 'four paragraphs' to represent that impact clearly.
rationales.conformity: He accepts that 'the rules here are different' and that if he doesn't tell Fernando what he has done, 'he genuinely won't know,' so he adapts to the professional norm.
rationales.tradition: He explicitly weighs his current behavior against what was proper 'where I grew up' and recalls how his mother would have viewed self-praise, showing strong reference to inherited cultural norms.
rationales.benevolence: He highlights sustained care for younger colleagues, with 'weekly one-on-ones' and PR reviews that shaped 'how two engineers think,' showing concrete investment in close others' growth.

rationales.hedonism: He marks the good news by taking 'the girls out for helado to celebrate,' deliberately choosing a simple pleasurable moment.
rationales.achievement: He is being put forward for the director role, and the scene centers on his strongest self-assessment and the team's reliability metrics being recognized.
rationales.conformity: He notes they are seeing 'a version of me that learned to write in their language' and accepts that adaptation as part of succeeding in the system.
rationales.benevolence: He holds back a correction because saying it 'would've turned a good moment into a philosophy seminar' and Camila 'deserved to just be happy for me,' prioritizing her experience.

rationales.achievement: He submits a full director packet, including endorsements and a restructuring proposal, showing active pursuit of advancement through visible competence.
rationales.security: He moderates the proposal because 'bold doesn't play well with everyone' on the review panel, carefully minimizing downside risk.
rationales.conformity: He frames criticism as 'building on existing strengths' instead of naming problems directly, tailoring his message to institutional expectations.

rationales.achievement: The entry is centered on a high-stakes panel interview where he delivers prepared answers to move forward professionally.
rationales.conformity: He says 'I said what I prepared. Didn't improvise, didn't overshoot,' showing deliberate restraint and adherence to expected interview behavior.

rationales.power: She explicitly draws the line with Andres, saying 'one more time and I'm finding someone else' and 'The gallery is mine and the standard is mine to set,' asserting authority over her space.
rationales.security: She acts because a late installer made her cover in front of a collector and she worries that 'that's exactly why I can't let things slide,' protecting business stability.
rationales.conformity: She enforces punctual, orderly behavior because she 'can't have my space looking disorganized when there's money on the table,' upholding clear professional norms.

rationales.achievement: She describes the finished triptych with pride, saying 'It works' and that rare moment when what she made matched what she envisioned, signaling successful execution of demanding work.
rationales.security: She emphasizes paying rent on time, having 'the next three months covered since January,' and updating her mother on the commission check, showing active focus on financial steadiness.

rationales.power: She lets the investor keep 'talking over me' and says she 'smiled and nodded through most of it instead of pushing back,' which shows a failure to assert control in an important negotiation.

rationales.self_direction: She values having a gallery where 'every corner of that space reflects a decision I made,' emphasizing independence in how the space is run.
rationales.power: She studies 'who commands a space' and says that when she had to negotiate every wall placement, nothing felt fully hers, showing strong concern with authority and control over the environment.

rationales.hedonism: She deliberately savors a 'Lazy Sunday,' sleeping late, making arepas, and sitting on the balcony enjoying the street sounds.
rationales.security: Even on a day off she reviews invoices because she likes 'knowing exactly where the money sits, what's coming in, what's going out,' showing active concern for predictability and reserves.
rationales.tradition: She connects her money-tracking instinct to family inheritance, recalling her father keeping 'a paper ledger for his tire shop,' which ties present choices to familial practice.

rationales.achievement: She successfully finalizes an exhibition lineup, takes a calculated risk on a new artist, and grounds it in collector commitments and strong March projections.
rationales.power: She says curating means 'I'm deciding whose career gets a platform and whose doesn't' and that 'I've earned that,' showing comfort with gatekeeping influence.
rationales.security: She notes 'the reserve fund is untouched, insurance renewal is handled' and that when finances are solid she can think clearly, highlighting deliberate protection of stability.

rationales.self_direction: She immediately pushes back with 'we need to clarify terms before anything moves forward,' refusing to let someone else define her arrangement for her.
rationales.power: Her reaction centers on title and authority, saying someone thinks they can 'slide into a title I haven't offered' and that 'the boundary is drawn,' which is a direct assertion of control.

rationales.achievement: The show preparation is executed cleanly, with artists delivering on time and the opening set exactly as planned.
rationales.power: She takes satisfaction that 'everything is exactly where I placed it' and that invitations went to 'the right people,' emphasizing control over both space and access.

rationales.achievement: She measures success through concrete wins like 'Sold four pieces in the first two hours' and a new artist sale on opening night.
rationales.power: A collector saying he'd 'been hearing my name' matters deeply to her, and she frames the moment as building a reputation in the city's art scene.
rationales.security: She stresses that 'the reserve is intact' and wants her mother to know the gallery is no longer 'some reckless bet,' showing concern with durable financial footing.

rationales.self_direction: She defends her independent path, noting she 'left a stable partnership to build this alone' and trusts her own judgment over a stranger's criticism.
rationales.power: She responds internally with 'My gallery, my choices, my track record,' centering ownership and control over the direction of the space.
rationales.security: She explicitly justifies her choices by 'keeping my lights on and my artists paid,' prioritizing viability and dependable operations.

rationales.power: She says of artist selection, 'I won't give that up,' making clear that decision authority is the core boundary in the negotiation.
rationales.security: Her hesitation is tied to funding risk, since 'I also can't fund the expansion alone and the bank terms are worse,' showing strong concern with practical stability.
